sanskrit: युञ्जन्नेवं सदात्मानं योगी नियतमानसः। शान्तिं निर्वाणपरमां मत्संस्थामधिगच्छति॥
transliteration: yuïjann evaà sadätmänaà yogé niyata-mänasaù çäntià nirväëa-paramäà mat-saàsthäm adhigacchati
literal_meaning: Thus / constantly / uniting / the self / the yogi / with regulated mind / peace / culminating in liberation / My abode / attains
contextual_meaning: When a person persistently integrates body, mind, and purpose under disciplined attention, the inner civil war ends and the self is experienced as already dwelling in a field of unbroken safety.
psychological_state: Post-integration homeostasis: the nervous system has shifted from threat-scanning to safe-connection mode, producing the subjective sense of arriving 'home' inside one's own skin.
trigger_scenario: After months of daily mindfulness practice, a burned-out executive notices that the usual morning dread is gone; instead of chasing the next goal, there is a quiet certainty of being exactly where one needs to be.
anchor_text: Keep body, mind, and purpose in steady union to rest in lasting peace.
